titleStmt.title: SII 38.203: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 203. (A.R. No. 203 of 1912.) TIRUVOṞṞIYŪR, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT. On the north wall, first prākāra, of the same temple.

p.hi: King: Sakalalōkachchakravarttigaḷ Year 7: Siṁha, ba. di. 2 (wrong for 12) Rājanārāyaṇa Sambhuvarāya (II) Thursday, Puṇar pūsam = 1344 A.D. August 5.

summary.p: This inscription records the meeting of the temple servants (śrīmāhēśvara) and trustees (tāṉttār), an individual Mudaliyār Saṅkaradēvar, (who is described as) an agent of Bhuvanēkabāhudēvar alias Kaliyuga Vīrabhadrar and two judges (nyāyattār), at a pavillion called Vyākarṇa-dānam tirukāvaṇam at Tiruvoṟṟiyūr. It is mentioned that in the past the temple at Tiruvoṟṟiyūr became a Muslim territory (turukkarrājyam) and was subjected to loot and plunder of its treasures by them. Those properties including lamp-stands, which had escaped their loot, were stolen by (the temple servants) Umaiyappiḷḷai, Magadarāyaṉ Tirukkaivēlamaḻagiyān, Pālaitten Sēdirāyaṉ Toṇḍaimāṉ, Chandran and others, who also committed many acts of sacrilege. As these culprits expired the above committee examined the charges and had decided to confiscate their lands and houses, after excluding those lands and houses which were auctioned earlier. Accordingly their lands totalling 24,690 kuḻi and houses were now ordered to be escheated to the temple as tirunāmattukkāṇi.

Further the record also states that one accountant Kaliugamayyaṉ, a mendicant (āṇḍār), who served as an account of another mendicant Ekāmbaradēvar, betrayed royal command (rāja-drōham) and was punished. His land of five thousand kuḻi of land with all its appurtenances and houses were bought for 350 paṇam and these properties were declared as tirunāmattukkāṇi of the temple.
Several persons including the offender Kaliyagamayyaṉ, Ekāmbaradēvaṉ and Prākramapāṇḍyapichchaṉ figure as signatories.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . sakalalokaccakravatti rājanārāyaṇaṉ campuvarāyarkku yāṇṭu Eḻāvatu ciṅkanāyaṟṟu Aparapakṣattu dvitiyaiyum viyāḻakkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa puṇarpūcattunāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu puḻalkoṭṭamāṉa vikramacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu
tiruvoṟṟiyūruṭaiyār tiruvoṟṟiyūruṭaiyanāyaṉārkoyil muṉṉāḷ turukkarirācciyamāyp pala cemaṅkaḷum ceytupoka Intac cemaṅkaḷ turukkar Eṭukkaiyil turukkaṟkut tappik kuttuviḷakkuk kāḷaviḷakku Uṭpaṭṭa cemam
tappikkiṭanta Inta cemattai Eṭuttu cilavaḻittuk koṇṭu tirukkoyilkaḷile palacivat�turākaṅkaḷumRead turokaṅkaḷumceyta civatturokikaḷai Ārāyntu tiracceyvatāka viyākaraṇatāṉam tirukkāvaṇattile cimākecurarum tāṉattārum kañcaṉūr
mutaliyār Aḻakiya tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyār makaṉār camaiyattār puvaṉekapākutevarāṉa kaliyuka virapattiratevar kāriyattukku kaṭava mutaliyār caṅkaratevarum Iraṇṭu niyThe letter �ya is engraved below the line as yāĀyattārum Iruntu Ivarkaḷai Ārāynta Aḷavil kūṟṟu ceyvār Umaiyappi
ḷḷaiyum makatarāyaṉ tirukkaivelamaḻakiyāṉum pālait teṉcetiyārāyaThe letter ya is engraved below the line.ṉum toṇṭaimāṉum cantiraṉum Uḷḷiṭṭār Ivarkaḷ kālaṟac cettuppoṉapaṭiyāle Ivarkaḷ maṉaiyuṅ kāṇiyumāyc caThe letter ca is engraved below the line.ṇṭecuraperuvilaiyāka viṟṟa maṉaikaḷ nikkiniṉṟa maṉaikaḷum kā
ṇiyumāy kalluveṭṭiṉa vaṭṭattil teruvil teṉciṟakil tiṟakka pāṭuvār maṉaikku kiḻakku ceṭṭikaḷmaṭattukku meṟku maṉaiyil vaṭaciṟakil meltalaiyil paricārakar maṉaikku vaṭakkuThe letter vaṭa is engraved below the line.kucaThe letter ca is engraved below the line.vaṉ maṉaikku teṟkku Oṉṟil puṟamillātāṉ tiruttoppu maṉaiyum viyākkiyā
ṉamaṇṭapattukku vaṭakku tiruvoṟṟiyūr Ammaimaṭattu Ellaikku teṟkup peṇṭukaḷ maṉai palavum Ivvūril vaṭakaraiyil Ivarkaḷ kāṇiyāṉa kampaṭipulamāṉa paṭampakka nāyaka viḷākam kuḻi 1000 3 100 3 10 5m karuppettamāṉa vaṭivuṭai maṅkaiviḷākam
kuḻi 1000 7 100 10 5m Āṉaikoṇṭāṉāṉa viyākaraṇatāṉa puṭaikuḻi 8 100 2 10m nuṅkāmpulamāṉa Ālālacuntaraviḷākam muṉṟil Oṉṟu kuḻi 9 100 7 10m poy pulaThe letter la is engraved below the line.māṉa Uṟavākkiṉaviḷākam muṉṟil Iraṇṭu kuḻi 1000 100 5 10m veḷḷarippulamaāṉa tirucciṟṟampala viḷākam kuḻi 1000 6 100m karuṅka
ḻiyāṉa kaliyuka virapattiraviḷākam kuḻiThe symbel kuḻi is engraved below the line. 1000 8 100 3 10 5m Iraṇṭu paṭṭiyāṉa caṇṭecuraviḷākam kuḻi 1000 100 8 10 5m melaiyettamāṉa supramaṇṇiyaThe word subrahmaṇṇiya is engraved below the line.viḷākam teṉpuṭai kuḻi 5 100 Āka paṉṉīraṭik kolāl kuḻi 10The letter ya is engraved below the line. 100 10m paḻantirunāmattukāṇi meṭṭettamāṉa tiruñāṉacampanta (viḷā)
viḷaākam kuḻi 1000 5 100 7 10m cāmpavāṉ Āṉa tirunāḷaippovāṉ viḷākam kuḻi 1000 2 100 2 10 Āka kuḻi 6 1000 7 100 9 10m māttuḻāṉāṉa tiruvoṟṟiyūr Ammaiviḷākam kuḻi 3 1000 7 100 9 10 Ākap paṉṉiraṭiyārThe letters p and ṉ are engraved below the line.kuḻi 10000 100 8 10 Āka kuḻi 2 10000 4 1000 6 100 9 10 Ikkuḻi Irupattu nālāyirattu Aṟunūṟṟu toṇṇu
ṟṟum Āka Inta vakaipaṭi nilamummaṉaikaḷum tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka cilālekai paṇṇiṉamaikkum Ivvāṇṭukku Etirāmāṇṭu Āṭimātam patinālān tiyati nāḷ Ivvūrk kālāṇṭārkaḷ tiruviti Āṇṭārkaḷ Āṇṭār Ēkāmparatevar Ivarkaḷ kaṇakkaril kaliukameyya
ṉ Irācatturokañ ceytu teṇṭam Ivarkaḷ pūṇkaiyil Ivarkaḷ kāṇi maṉaiyum viḷai nilamumāy Ivarkaḷ tirunāmattukkāṇi Āka viṟṟukkuṭutta vaṭakkut tiruvitiyil vaṭaciṟakil tiruvoṟṟiyūrnāyakar maṉai Iraṇṭum teṉciṟakil Ēkāmparatevar maṉai Iraṇ
ṭum paṇippeṇṭukaḷ teruvil meykkāppāṉ maṉaiāyp paṇṭāramāṉa maṉaikku vaṭakku maṉai kolnālum teṉkaraiyil nanneti puṭainālu Itil teṉpāti puṭai Iraṇṭiṉāl kuḻi catam nellikiḻāṉ puṭai Iraṇṭiṉāl kuḻi catam paruttimeṭu vaṭapuṭai tirunāmattu
kkāṇi Ākaiyil teṉpuṭai kuḻi 1000 Āka 5 1000 kuḻi Ikkuḻi Aiyyāyiramum nirum niroṭu kālum maramum potuvum Uṭpaṭavum Immaṉaikaḷ Uṭpaṭavum Āka Ivvakaippaṭikku vilai Āṉa paṇam 3 100 5 10 Ippaṇam muṉṉūṟṟuaimpatum kuṭuttut tirunāmattukkāṇi Āka ko
ṇṭu cilālekai paṇṇiṉamaikku Ivai jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalapiṭāraṉ Eḻuttu Ivai tavaneṟipeṟṟār Eḻuttu Ippaṭikku Ivai kaliukameyyaṉ Eḻuttu Ippaṭikku Ivai Ēkāmparatevaṉ Eḻuttu Ivai tiruvoṟṟiyūrppiḷḷai Eḻuttu Ippaṭikku Ivai parākkiṟa
mapāṇṭiyappiccaṉ Eḻuttu ..
Digital edition of SII 38.203 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
